name: Timespinner
description: Timespinner is a beautiful metroidvania inspired by classic 90s action-platformers.

options.label: Death Link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies.

options.label: Progression Balancing
options.description: A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

options.label: Accessibility
options.description: Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

options.label: Start With Jewelry Box
options.description: Start with Jewelry Box unlocked

options.label: Downloadable Items
options.description: With the tablet you will be able to download items at terminals

options.label: Eye Spy
options.description: Requires Oculus Ring in inventory to be able to break hidden walls.

options.label: Start With Meyef
options.description: Start with Meyef, ideal for when you want to play multiplayer.

options.label: Quick Seed
options.description: Start with Talaria Attachment, Nyoom!

options.label: Specific Keycards
options.description: Keycards can only open corresponding doors

options.label: Inverted
options.description: Start in the past

options.label: Gyre Archives
options.description: Gyre locations are in logic.

options.label: Cantoran
options.description: Cantoran's fight and check are available upon revisiting his room

options.label: Lore Checks
options.description: Memories and journal entries contain items.

options.label: Boss Rando
options.description: Wheter all boss locations are shuffled, and if their damage/hp should be scaled.

options.label: Boss Rando Type
options.description: Sets what type of boss shuffling occurs.

options.label: Enemy Rando
options.description: Wheter enemies will be randomized, and if their damage/hp should be scaled.

options.label: Damage Rando
options.description: Randomly nerfs and buffs some orbs and their associated spells as well as some associated rings.

options.label: Hp Cap
options.description: Sets the number that Lunais's HP maxes out at.

options.label: Aura Cap
options.description: Sets the maximum Aura Lunais is allowed to have.

options.label: Level Cap
options.description: Sets the max level Lunais can achieve.

options.label: Extra Earrings Xp
options.description: Adds additional XP granted by Galaxy Earrings.

options.label: Boss Healing
options.description: Enables/disables healing after boss fights.

options.label: Shop Fill
options.description: Sets the items for sale in Merchant Crow's shops.

options.label: Shop Warp Shards
options.description: Shops always sell warp shards (when keys possessed), ignoring inventory setting.

options.label: Shop Multiplier
options.description: Multiplier for the cost of items in the shop.

options.label: Loot Pool
options.description: Sets the items that drop from enemies (does not apply to boss reward checks) Vanilla: Drops are the same as the base game Randomized: Each slot of every enemy's drop table is given a random use item or piece of equipment.

options.label: Drop Rate Category
options.description: Sets the drop rate when 'Loot Pool' is set to 'Random' Tiered: Based on item rarity/value Vanilla: Based on bestiary slot the item is placed into Random: Assigned a random tier/drop rate Fixed: Set by the 'Fixed Drop Rate' setting

options.label: Fixed Drop Rate
options.description: Base drop rate percentage when 'Drop Rate Category' is set to 'Fixed'

options.label: Loot Tier Distro
options.description: Sets how often items of each rarity tier are placed when 'Loot Pool' is set to 'Random' Default Weight: Rarer items will be assigned to enemy drop slots less frequently than common items Full Random: Any item has an equal chance of being placed in an enemy's drop slot Inverted Weight: Rarest items show up the most frequently, while common items are the rarest

options.label: Show Bestiary
options.description: All entries in the bestiary are visible, without needing to kill one of a given enemy first

options.label: Show Drops
options.description: All item drops in the bestiary are visible, without needing an enemy to drop one of a given item first

options.label: Enter Sandman
options.description: The Ancient Pyramid is unlocked by the Twin Pyramid Keys, but the final boss door opens if you have all 5 Timespinner pieces

options.label: Dad Percent
options.description: The win condition is beating the boss of Emperor's Tower

options.label: Rising Tides
options.description: Random areas are flooded or drained, can be further specified with RisingTidesOverrides

options.label: Unchained Keys
options.description: Start with Twin Pyramid Key, which does not give free warp; warp items for Past, Present, (and ???

options.label: Back To The Future
options.description: When inverted, allows using the refugee camp warp when both the Timespinner Wheel and Spindle is acquired.

options.label: Prism Break
options.description: Adds 3 Laser Access items to the item pool to remove the lasers blocking the military hangar area instead of needing to beat the Golden Idol, Aelana, and The Maw.

options.label: Lock Key Amadeus
options.description: Lasers in Amadeus' Laboratory are disabled via items, rather than by de-powering the lab.

options.label: Risky Warps
options.description: Expanded free-warp eligible locations, including Azure Queen, Xarion, Amadeus' Laboratory, and Emperor's Tower.

options.label: Pyramid Start
options.description: Start in ???.

options.label: Gate Keep
options.description: The castle drawbridge starts raised, and can be lowered via item.

options.label: Royal Roadblock
options.description: The Royal Towers entrance door requires a royal orb (Plasma Orb, Plasma Geyser, or Royal Ring) to enter.

options.label: Pure Torcher
options.description: All lanterns contain checks.

options.label: Find The Flame
options.description: Lanterns in 'Pure Torcher' will not break without new item 'Cube of Bodie'.

options.label: Trap Chance
options.description: Chance of traps in the item pool.

options.label: Traps
options.description: List of traps that may be in the item pool to find
